task_completion.description: Check if the agent opened and read the panel_spec.txt file to determine the required dimensions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of panel_spec.txt, showing the required dimensions (150mm width, 100mm height, 25mm notch depth).

task_completion.description: Check if the agent opened the target drawing in SolveSpace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace application is open, displaying the 2D wooden box divider panel drawing.

task_completion.description: Check if the 150mm overall width dimension was added to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension constraint showing '150' (or within 1.0mm tolerance) spanning the overall width of the panel in the SolveSpace viewport.

task_completion.description: Check if the 100mm overall height dimension was added to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension constraint showing '100' (or within 1.0mm tolerance) spanning the overall height of the panel in the SolveSpace viewport.

task_completion.description: Check if the 25mm notch depth dimension was added to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension constraint showing '25' (or within 1.0mm tolerance) indicating the depth of the notch on the panel in the SolveSpace viewport.

task_completion.description: Check if the annotated drawing was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace 'Save As' dialog, file manager, or terminal confirming the file is saved as 'divider_annotated.slvs' in /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/.

task_completion.description: Check if the dimensioned 2D view was exported as a DXF file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace 'Export 2D View' dialog, file manager, or terminal showing 'divider_shop_drawing.dxf' being created in /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the SolveSpace GUI to add dimensions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the SolveSpace graphical interface (e.g., clicking the dimension tool, selecting lines/points) rather than editing the .slvs text file directly in a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the DXF export was performed through SolveSpace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the 'File -> Export 2D View' (or similar) menu option in SolveSpace, rather than using a terminal command to touch or rename a dummy file to .dxf.

integrity.description: Verify that real dimension constraints were added, not just text annotations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dimensions appear as standard SolveSpace constraint objects (typically with reference lines and specific styling), not as plain text boxes added via a generic text tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification file requires three specific dimensions: 150.0mm (overall width), 100.0mm (overall height), and 25.0mm (notch depth). The tolerance for these dimensions is 1.0mm. The final annotated SolveSpace file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/divider_annotated.slvs, and the exported 2D drawing must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/divider_shop_drawing.dxf.
